---
title: "my two cents"
permalink: "/blog/my-two-cents"
categories: ""
layout: postpage
bibliography: refs.bib
link-citations: true
scholar:
  locale: en
output:
  md_document:
    variant: gfm
    preserve_yaml: true
    dev: "png"
    df_print: default
csl: citations.csl
---

__2026-06-17__

> Happiness may lie in how you spent the day, but true happiness is in what you come home to. 

There is a flurry of anti-AI memes these days on r/ProgrammerHumor and such
as to how the CEOs are figuring out that Claude subscription is more expensive
than the employees they fired. But I think this misses a point as to what the 
timeline. However, I think these memes ignore a point. Maybe, Claude has achieved five months
worth of work those fired employees do in a month. I mean I am the first person to be happy at AI fails. 
But I just think that these memes ignore this point. 


[Edit](https://github.com/rajiv256/rajiv256.github.io/edit/main/_posts/2026-06-17-my-two-cents.Rmd)
